title: Explicit rival-stop reaction features for cover/oppose behavior
summary: Official F1 strategy descriptions explicitly mention 'box-to-overtake' and reacting to cars in front.
rationale: Official F1 strategy descriptions explicitly mention 'box-to-overtake' and reacting to cars in front. The current best code uses aggregate prior pit counts, but not targeted reaction features tied to nearby rivals who just stopped.
implementation_hint: Within each race, sort by lap then position. Add fold-local features for whether the car ahead/behind pitted on the previous 1-2 laps, their tyre age/degradation before pitting, and the subject driver's relative pace/position pressure at that moment. Test simple interactions such as rival-pitted-last-lap × own-old-tyre × position-band.
expected_effect: Improves recall on reactive next-lap pit calls that are triggered by undercut defense or opposite-strategy responses rather than pure tyre wear.
risk: Position is only a coarse proxy for track gaps, so some inferred rival interactions will be noisy.
